sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Carson City, Lake Tahoe and tributaries
sources.quotedText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries    Open year around    Daily: 5 game fish    Possession: 10 game fish    1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset

sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16 — Carson City CLOSED WATERS (none listed)
Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Identity: OpenStreetMap maps this small stream as way 543437017, tagged simply name='Secret' (waterType creek); 'Secret Creek' is used here as the conventional full name and the OSM label is retained as an alias. It is a west-slope Carson Range stream flowing northwest toward the Secret Harbor / Secret Cove area of the Nevada east shore of Lake Tahoe (mapped reach from about 39.13022, -119.90808 down to 39.13985, -119.92390), placing it in the Lake Tahoe drainage in Carson City County (Western Region). NDOW does not publish a dedicated waters page for this stream (the ndow.org 'Secret Creek' page is a different Secret Creek in Elko County), but as a Carson City Lake Tahoe tributary it is governed by the Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing in CR 25-16: open year-round, with restricted hours and a 5/10 game-fish limit. This specific sub-listing overrides the Carson City general 'All waters' default (5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish daily; any hour). Carson City has no closed waters in CR 25-16, so no part of this creek is closed. verifyCurrent is set true because the water's identity and county rest on OSM/geocoding rather than an NDOW waters record. This is the Nevada rule; it differs from the California §7.50(b)(152) seasonal catch-and-release closure that applies to Tahoe tributaries on the California side of the lake.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Carson City — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round as a Lake Tahoe tributary (Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing); Carson City lists no closed waters.
regulations.verbatimText: Lake Tahoe and tributaries. Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: The Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing sets 5 game fish daily and 10 in possession, with no species sub-limit. 'Game fish' in Nevada includes trout, mountain whitefish, kokanee salmon, bass, and other listed game species combined.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Carson City — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: 5 game fish daily, 10 in possession (Lake Tahoe and tributaries listing; no species sub-limit).
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 game fish    Possession: 10 game fish

parameters.note: The Carson City 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing restricts fishing hours to 1 hour before sunrise through 2 hours after sunset (unlike Carson City's general 'All waters' default, which allows fishing any hour of the day or night).

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Carson City — Lake Tahoe and tributaries)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset

parameters.note: Nevada requires a fishing license for residents and nonresidents 12 years of age or older; anglers under 12 need no license (nonresidents under 12 are limited to 50% of the daily bag). The California/Nevada Lake Tahoe license reciprocity (and the Nevada Interstate Boundary Waters License) applies to fishing IN Lake Tahoe itself, not to its tributaries above the lake — a Nevada fishing license is required to fish this creek.

regulations.humanSummary: Nevada fishing license required for anglers 12+ (Lake Tahoe license reciprocity does not extend to the tributary).
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
